item.dc:creator: Grogu007

item.title: Pride Postin' 2025 or How Transitioning Saved My Life
item.description:
Hey, Ya'll! Similar to my post from two prides ago, I want to share my timeline and story for those who may be in doubt, or need some reassurance. I'm pretty introverted so pride parades/events were never really my scene. This feels like the best way I can support my community, especially now as transgender people are under attack all over the world by growing right wing extremism. Ultimately, the conceit of this post is to give people hope that there is a good life on the other side, and give ya'll someone to reach out to if you need it.
This was me at the 1 year mark. I saw so many positive changes, and I moved across the country finally unafraid. I had a lot more changes to go through than maybe some others, and less than others. It wasn't until the 2 - 2 1/2 year mark that I really felt like I started to come into my own identity.
This was the photo I used for my last pride post - Left: about 2-2 1/2 years in, Right: 2016 maybe? I still think this was the best I ever looked pre-transition. New stuff: I had a small amount of surgery on the top half of my face in June 2024. I took 3 months off of work. The FMLA process is very confusing on purpose and you have to wrangle doctors while recovering which makes the whole thing incredibly stressful. It was stressful and I had a minor complication but my doctors were very patient with me the whole way through, even with my panicked emails. Healing took longer than I wanted and they gave me the worst haircut of my life. I'm glad I did it because most of my dysphoria has been taken care of by that point with those minimal tweaks. Voice therapy sucks ass and I'm still struggling with it after years of giving it a mostly earnest try. I'm preparing for bottom surgery but otherwise living my life without thinking so hard about myself and my identity. (OW. Getting lasers shot at your nethers is unpleasant.) Also I look so much MORE like my sister now and we already regularly got asked if we were twins before. The end for now! I'm here if you need an ear~

item.title: Return of the Meme: 1370 (fabulous tragedy)
item.description:
Let's get this bread.
Just no.
The wonderful Shenanigans that Ukraine pulled with the drones is some Hannibal Barca marching over the goddamn alps level shit and it's amazing.
Is Slavic rural life, yes?
Ohhh boy. One of the most, uh, colorful pieces of Roman poetry, that's for sure.
I believe this is from Jim'll Paint It.
I do as the post commands.
I love that the Macaw has a coconut drink. I hope it's the cocktail known as a Jungle Bird.
This is one of the best summations I've ever heard of Sedona.
Big @kaeldra energy
Archer is me. I have no idea what my blood type is.
I really wanna know what the Attic Greek word that they're translating as fabulous is here.
I'm so tired of this bullshit.
Well, I guess nobody likes me, then. I ALWAYS bring a book to the bar.
Birds of Paradise, as a whole, are some of the most batshit of living dinosaurs in terms of how they look and prove that their ancestors probably looked more bizarre than we can ever hope to imagine.
Also big @kaeldra energy.
Chat GPT can't help you find shit.
Huh, I wonder why you're getting assaulted so much.
I recommend this racoon as our new national symbol.
Me, uploading this post and doing the first round of commentary at 12:07 AM Arizona time.
Why? Food poisoning. AGAIN. what the damn hell. This time from my first Pizza in an age. Looks like to be safe and paranoid, I'm not going to eat anything that I didn't prepare myself for a while. Ugh.
I did get back to sleep for a bit and dreamed of finding a vein of Lapis Lazuli, though.
And Billions of Passenger Pigeons. I wonder what that would have been like to see. Now both are gone.
Pippin tax. Today's message from Pippin: [existential conure screams] ______ 1) Are you on the run to a safe place and passing through Arizona on your way to safer grounds? My apartment is open to you to crash at, and it is a safe space. Pippin and I will make you a meal and gladly share tea, wine, or mead with you. 2) Hey! If you're a woman or minority in a STEM field in Arizona living north of Phoenix and you need new headshots for this website or another detailing your experiences/research/accomplishment because our monstrous regime has deleted your experience from an official government website, PM me. Pippin and I have a good Nikon DSLR, and we'll do the photos for free, and, uh, you'll get to meet Pippin, I guess? (If you know someone who could use this who's not on Imgur, feel free to PM me to get my email address for them and tell them they're free to message me.) 3) Take your meds with a full glass of water, please. Do it for Pippin, or Space Mom, or better yet, do it for yourself. 4) Ĝefeloað wē. We will carry on. 5) all funds from the sales of items at these two links will be sent to support charities aiding to fight for Trans rights in the US (not just T-shirts!) https://www.teepublic.com/t-shirt/74812801-all-conures-are-anarchists https://www.teepublic.com/t-shirt/74811778-whiptail-pride

item.title: I was today years old when I learned...
item.description:
Came across this guy's (GenericArtDad) video, and it left me confused. I'm with him. That IS I what I thought empathy was, too. So if it's not that to everyone, what is it?
Ah, right off the bat, this definition of empathy definitely illustrates where some autistic people may struggle and why you may hear the idea that we lack empathy. Microexpressions, what are thoooose.
Welp, yeah. This has truly opened a world of perspective for me. Holy fak. I'm useless without tons of context and details (bottom up thinker, y'all), and that absolutely contributes to deficits in cognitive empathy.
This site gives the following example of these types of empathy in action: Your friend has endured a heartbreak. The significant other was not a good influence on your friend, however, so although you feel with your friend and feel sad (affective empathy), a part of you is also happy, as your friend’s future is likely to become more positive. But note that this happiness is your own perspective on her predicament. Your friend’s mindset doesn’t yet understand that the breakup is something positive, as she is focused on the loss of her partner. She feels heartbroken, alone, and possibly even rejected. You understand that your friend’s pain is temporary, that it will be gone soon, and that she will be happier in the end. But this is sympathy, not empathy. But you understand your friend’s mental state, which entails the heartbreak, the loss, and the sense of rejection (cognitive empathy). If you were to show sympathy devoid of empathy, you would tell your friend that the breakup is a good thing. As such, you would fail to validate her, and in fact, your friend will feel misunderstood by you, on top of feeling rejected by her ex. This is an example of either your cognitive empathy or affective empathy failing. You understand the situation is hard for her and are moved to help (compassionate empathy), but you can not help her properly if not all aforementioned types of empathy inform your actions https://embrace-autism.com/the-different-types-of-empathy/ I thought that my deficit was the inability to properly express sympathy, which has always felt superficial and performative to me. I would generally feel what they felt by putting myself in their shoes and express a similar situation where I felt the same. I put myself in their shoes and sat in their emotions with them. The compassion part is harder, I don't know what to do or say to help half the time. I can give practical advice, but that may not be what they're looking for. (People who preface if they're looking to vent or looking for solutions are angels BTW) There is also another empathy expression called motor empathy, which is an automatic response where one mirrors the other. Think yawn contagion. I experience this seeing others cry. You cry. I'm gonna cry. I may not know how you're feeling looking at you, but I can put myself in your situation and know how that would affect me and how it makes me feel. It also explains why I struggle so hard in interpersonal relationships. Feeling your feelings and mine is a lot! This is also why I think I have struggled making friends with other women. Women are so emotionally available with each other and seem to be highly cognitively empathetic. I struggle to meet them on their level in that regard. This has given a new layer to the double empathy problem between NTs and NDs that I need to ponder on.

item.title: Oh, you're here! The tour is about to start! Let me take you on a journey...
item.description:
Theodoros Rallis 1852 - 1909 At the bath (aka as Bain Arabe), ca. 1905
Eugène de Blaas 1843 - 1932 Awaiting the Return
Edwin Long 1829 - 1891 Queen Esther, 1878
Eleuterio Pagliano (Italian painter) 1826 - 1903 Giocando a Volano (Playing Badminton), 1876
Vittorio Gussoni 1893 - 1968 Giovane Donna con Vassaio di Frutta (Young Woman with Fruit Tray)
Francois Alfred Delobbe 1835 - 1920 Woman Sifting, 1882
Gaetano Bellei 1857 - 1922 Colpi di Vento (Gust of wind), 1920
John William Godward 1861 - 1922 Mischief and Repose, 1909
Laura Coombs Hills 1859 - 1952 Fire Opal (Grace Mutell), 1899
William Sergeant Kendall 1869 - 1938 Woman with Parrot
Charles Landelle 1812 - 1908 Young girl from Tetouan, Morocco
Edwin Long 1829 - 1891 Thisbe, 1881
Jane Maria Bowkett 1837 - 1891 Young Lady in a Conservatory
Leonard Campbell Taylor 1874 - 1969 Far Away Thoughts
William Henry Margetson 1861 - 1940 Fresh Lavender, 1909
Carl Budtz-Moller 1882 - 1953 Sunlit Interior from Melsted on Bornholm, 1924
Arthur Wardle 1864 - 1949 Arctic Encounter, s.d.
William McGregor Paxton 1869 - 1941 Girl with the Sea Beyond
William Orpen 1878 - 1931 The Window Seat, 1901
Carl Larsson 1853 - 1919 Woman Lying on a Bench, 1913
Peder Severin Krøyer 1851 - 1909 Sommeraften ved Skagen (Summer Evening at Skagen), 1892
Giovanni Boldini 1842 - 1931 Nudo femminile
Valentine Thomas Garland 1840 - 1914 Cat's cradle, 1899

item.title: Scarlett Witch & the push up challenge!
item.description:
Love being in this cosplay!
It's the chaotic cosplayer who does stupid things like doing 3,214 push-ups in 23 days as part of The Push-Up Challenge to raise awareness and funds for mental health in Australia. This target represents the 3,214 people who died by suicide in Australia in 2023 (the most recent stats). You can push for better mental health too by donating to my fundraising page and supporting this important cause: www.thepushupchallenge.com.au/fundraisers/krissiq81114/the-push-up-challenge Funds raised will go to Headspace National to support much-needed mental health services and initiatives in Australia. Any donation, big or small, is really appreciated, last year we raised over $400! Xx tia
item.dc:creator: Krissiq

item.title: What Happened Today #123
item.description: The institutions are fighting back against authoritarian moves more than ever. Federal judges block Trump's mass layoffs and union-busting while governors demand answers about ICE targeting honor students. Corporate America abandons law firms that bent the knee, students walk out in protest, and congressional Democrats call the administration "fascist" as senior federal workers resign rather than stay silent. When judges, governors, businesses, workers, and students all refuse to comply, authoritarianism starts to crack, but only if we back them up. Meanwhile, don't rely on the algorithm. Let me send this free and private summary to you every night so that you never miss what's going on. Add your email on the homepage of GovBrief.today #GovBriefToday #Resist 1. https://go.govbrief.today/trump-no-enrichment 2. https://go.govbrief.today/wednesday-tariff-deadline 3. https://go.govbrief.today/ca-tariff-suit-dismissed 4. https://go.govbrief.today/noaa-hiring-meterologists 5. https://go.govbrief.today/alaska-drilling-mining 6. https://go.govbrief.today/pentagon-greenland-alignment 7. https://go.govbrief.today/counter-wmd-office 8. https://go.govbrief.today/california-trans-athletes 9. https://go.govbrief.today/soliman-hate-crime 10. https://go.govbrief.today/border-agents-diversity 11. https://go.govbrief.today/fema-administrator-hurricane 12. https://go.govbrief.today/marcelo-gomes-jailed 13. https://go.govbrief.today/nadler-aide-investigation 14. https://go.govbrief.today/more-afrikaners-arrive 15. https://go.govbrief.today/harvard-summary-judgment 16. https://go.govbrief.today/judge-blocks-tsa-union-dissolution 17. https://go.govbrief.today/trump-scotus-firings Yesterday's Top Story - https://go.govbrief.today/shattuck-everyday-resistance Fighting Back - https://go.govbrief.today/companies-abandon-law-firms

item.title: Yosemite Falls In Yosemite National Park California
item.description: Yosemite Falls is the highest waterfall in Yosemite National Park, dropping a total of 2,425 feet (739 m) from the top of the upper fall to the base of the lower fall. Located in the Sierra Nevada of California, it is a major attraction in the park, especially in late spring when the water flow is at its peak. #mountains #awesome #nature #hiking #the_great_outdoors
item.dc:creator: OceansRust

item.title: He took his grandma to see the Isle Of Man TT for the first time
item.dc:creator: NuclearMam

item.description:
.... I'm just so sad. Constantly on the verge of tears. I'm half native. I didn't want to hijack anyone else's memorial post, but I had a bit more to say... Last week, I finished watching 1923 on Paramount+. I have very mixed feelings about the show overall, but it does have, from any media that I've seen, the most accurate portrayal of racism against natives, in particular government sanctioned Catholic boarding schools designed to eradicate our culture, and the maliciousness of the 'justice system' when mobilized against us or just indifference if it is not.
The first season of the show is legit some of the best TV I've ever seen. The start of the 2nd season however, begins with an In Memoriam for one youngest native actors. Colin Brings Plenty.
So of course, I had to investigate what could have happened to such a young guy. https://www.latimes.com/entertainment-arts/tv/story/2024-11-18/cole-brings-plenty-1923-actor-death-investigation He was a native activist, was found dead under what everyone except the police/justice department would think would be pretty suspicious circumstances... with no investigation... There's already more than enough info on here to identify me if anyone really, really cared to dig, so here's some more messed up family history for y'all. Since I was around twelve, I've constantly been around some adults that like to have on shows like
or Criminal Minds, NCIS, CSI (these are the ones I'd actually leave the room for, except for CSI: Miami, he's just too dumb not to stick around laugh, they'd mostly not watch it with me around because they didn't want it to be a comedy, lol).... in all of these types of shows, investigations in movies, etc. it's drilled into our heads... most crimes are unmeditated crimes of passion, committed by someone they know, often very well... in cases of death... it's almost always, the boyfriend/girlfriend/husband/wife/partner did it.... Almost all cases I guess...
My youngest sister was found shot in the chest on the reservation... and it was just ruled a suicide... no investigation into her boyfriend who was working as a security guard at the casino on the reservation at the time... who became a police officer on the reservation shortly after... my aunt who had adopted that sister (when she was two) moved off the reservation because of that... it is no longer a safe place for our family... But our tribe has a treaty with the state of Minnesota, we sold our hunting and fishing rights, and every year each enrolled member gets a per capita check, so once a year I get to feel like this...
Back to the show I was watching. Don't worry, we're getting to close to the end...
Despite my glowing recommendation of the first season, and my respect for the potrayal of native racism, I've never been so upset when I've finished a show... and I've watched a lot of shows over the years folks...
A gradual degradation isn't too upsetting....
Neither is being yanked along weirdness that you figure out that writers didn't really know what to do with either....
Some sharper declines in quality at the end are definitely more upsetting...
Some of the declines are pretty bad.
Some so sharp... and so bad that just collectively... it *DEFINITELY* ended at X point.... But 1923.... The first season is some of the best episodes of anything I've watched, and they just use your hope from that to keep you in the train wreck of the second... it's like the second season was with the same actors playing stupider versions of their characters, several people made dumb decisions they'd never done anything like before that got them killed... So many gratuitous brainwashing/torture scenes... yes... we get that the evil guy is evil, you don't need to keep showing it, I'm trying to watch a historical drama not Hostel... for fuck's sack... So... it's definitely an experience... and one I'm hesitant to actually recommend to anyone... it was recommended to me by my mother-in-law and I have very mixed feelings about it... how would a black person feel about being recommended to watch 12 Years a Slave by a non-black relative/relation?
In the 70's my grandpa was found dead in a swamp. They ruled that he drowned because he was drunk and found in a swamp. But the autopsy showed that there was no water in his lungs... That's... definitely not how that works... When I was twelve, I moved in with my aunt and uncle, I went from a very sheltered life to a pretty free one. That's when I started consuming media voraciously. I also have a sensory processing disorder, there is no background noise function for me, so if it's on... I will hear it, and they'd watch The Simpsons and King of the Hill.... so after the week of trying to come to terms to with 1923 and family history it's brought up, I get on here expecting to see a bunch of nice pets and good start to pride month....
and... RIP John Redcorn
it's been good weather, but rainy at will for me for since finding out...
I was in tears even before finding out the horrific full story, and since then, waterworks available on command... I'm just so sad, and so tired... I hope he's high enough profile to get justice, and that there's enough visibility on this, but they're already on the defensive to cover up that they let this happen... I'm half native. This is a gay native man, who they already realized that they screwed up and let this happen to someone who *a lot* of people would see...
I hope I care enough to get you guys more pixels next time... From watching an accurate portrayal of the racism over a hundred years ago... to the hate crimes still happening today...
why...
Unlike this post.
I know pet tax is typical for a lengthy post, but it doesn't feel right ending this on a good note. It shouldn't.
item.dc:creator: ICorrectGrammarIncorrectlyForAmusement

item.title: Because it's important: a reminder of what the growing cells in the first 2-3 months of pregnancy actually look like
item.description: The forced birthers have successfully spread images of miniaturised babies as representation of what is happening in the womb in early pregnancy, when that couldn't be further from the truth. There isn't even a fetus yet at that point, let alone anything remotely resembling a human (or any being, period). Their tactic was and is to rile up an emotional response when telling lies, pushing through violent legislation, and pressuring and forcing people with a uterus to carry a pregnancy to term. There is no mini human at that point. There are just cells with the potential to grow into something that *then* has the potential to grow into a human. Having the choice to keep or to terminate is of utmost importance. And knowing what there actually exists during the relevant time is part of that choice.
item.dc:creator: abetteridiot

item.dc:creator: tampacl

item.title: An example of the reasoning disconnect in groups searching for evidence of Vaccine death. This posted by BCRail, suffering from a lack of cognitive ability.
item.description:
So here it is: a study by a group that peer reviews itself showing exceptionally poor conclusions from questionable, non-present, or fabricated data and reviewed by themselves rather than independent peers: https://publichealthpolicyjournal.com/a-systematic-review-of-autopsy-findings-in-deaths-after-covid-19-vaccination/
As if a facebook link isnt the first clue this might be a sketchy source of review, lets look on. Medline doesnt mention any of the 3 journals the 'peers' are utilizing, along with the journal authors here, meaning it probably already isnt anything credible, but second, lacking publications in any of NEJIM, NIH, CJM or BMJ likely is because their definition of peer reviewed is thus as they get rejected from any credible journal. It isnt worth the waste of paper its' been printed on, in all probability. The critical thinking anyone with more than 3 firing brain cells needs to do is: not just to seek the OUTCOME you expect, but to look at science with a reputation for independent integrety and peer review. Moving on though, lets examine the claim above. 73.9% ! GOSH ! Thats a MAJORITY. But of what? Sudden deaths of WHAT GROUP STUDIED? The offered source holds this paper: https://publichealthpolicyjournal.com/a-systematic-review-of-autopsy-findings-in-deaths-after-covid-19-vaccination/ "We initially identified 678 studies and, after screening for our inclusion criteria, included 44 papers that contained 325 autopsy cases and one organ-restricted autopsy case (heart)." Which studies, and by what criteria did they pick 44 of the papers with the 325 autopsy cases they selected. Selectively. For the selection of the outcome they wanted. Confirmation bias, much? Im not going to break down WHY this seems to be, as a lot of nonsense is conveyed. It contradicts what other reputable publications have discovered through real peer reviewed science, so Ill leave the rest of that reading to you and instead back up my claim with real peer reviewed science. Specifically that on clinically verified deaths due to the COVID vaccine. Now this will be a low number, because some pople had other co-morbidities at time of death (other things wrong with them that COVID or a negative reaction, if any, to the vaccine or any other reason may have pushed them over the edge and caused death), but even if the real number is 10 or 100 times what we think we know it is (~55/8 BILLION) MINISCULE in relation to how many doses have been administered. Sauce: https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC8875435/ There are other studies, and you can look up those at NEJIM or anywhere else, but they find similar results so lets just run with this one. NIH has been counting deaths reported to coroner and from hospitals across the world and they found that out of 8 BILLION doses administered, 55 cases can be concretely tied to vaccine dosage. If you have a problem with the number - if its 55, 5500, or 55,000 , compared to the billions of doses given, its LESS than one percent, and by a good margin. Now, you HAVE to assess risk every day. Going out your front door, even, but you DO IT ANWAYS because its WORTH IT. The vaccine lowers symtoms and SAVES LIVES in OVER 99.999% of the people vaccinated. A lot better than the %0.7 to 3.5% chance of DEATH in clinical cases for COVID. Its like wearing your seatbelt. Can it kill you? YES ! REMOTELY ! But it has a FAR GERATER chance of SAVING YOUR LIFE in a crash if you wear it. This is the fault in reasoning with people taking a singular focus to validate their fear. The *IS* a chance of adverse reaction to vaccine. The error is assuming there isnt a FAR GREATER risk of harm from these diseases. This isnt a common cold or mild flu. Measeles vaccine is worth taking because of high infant mortality. Scarlet fever vaccine is worth taking to avoid heart damage. Mumps, Rubella, Diptheria and others are taken as their risks are far outwieghed by the consequences of catching their respective diseases and SO IS COVID. Unplug yourself from the facebook disinformation cabal and seek a better light. Get a more established (before 2020 as PHPJ is) and assure it is a REPUTABLE, independently peer reviewed publication, and then read MORE. Speak to MORE than one doctor. Dont just turn away when you dont find what you seek but look at all of them and then how the conclusions contradict or reinforce themselves. I wrote this book not just for @BCRail , but for everyone who is fearful of vaccines. Check your assessment of risk, and I think you can find some comfort and reassurance that it is, indeed the proper method to take versus the horrible diseases that assail us.
item.dc:creator: Bystandr

item.title: 'Traditional' work motivation
item.dc:creator: AdventurousDonut480
